items.situation: two students chatting about a part-time job one just started at a factory

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, I heard you started that part-time job at the chocolate factory. How's it going?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's good, actually! But my shift starts at five in the morning, so I'm always exhausted by afternoon.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Five a.m.? Yikes. That's brutal with classes too.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, the early start is rough. The pay's great, though, and they let me leave by ten, so I still make my lectures.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, well, if you're that tired, maybe you should drop your eight-thirty seminar and take the evening section instead.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You know, that's a really good idea. I'll talk to my advisor about switching this week.

questions.stem: What does the man say is difficult about his new job?

options.A: His shift begins very early in the morning
options.B: The factory is far from campus
options.C: The pay is lower than he expected
options.D: He often has to work late at night

questions.explanation: 男士明确说他的班次早上五点开始，所以总是很累，A正确。B距离从未被提及，属于related_but_not_stated。C与对话相反，男士说工资很高，属于wrong_detail。D把时间弄反了，他是早班并十点前下班，属于wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do this week?

options.A: Quit his job at the factory
options.B: Ask for a later shift at work
options.C: Talk to his advisor about changing a class
options.D: Sign up for an extra morning seminar

questions.explanation: 男士最后说要和导师谈谈换到晚间班次的课，C正确。A过度推断，他没说要辞职且说工资很好，属于over_inference。B无对话依据，他没打算改班次而是改课，属于unrelated_next_step。D与情境相反，他想避开早上的课，属于rejected_option。

items.situation: two roommates working out a fair chore and quiet-hours schedule

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So now that we're rooming together, I think we should sort out chores and a quiet-hours rule before it becomes a problem.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. I'm a night owl, so I usually study till about one a.m. Does that bother you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: A little. I have early labs, so I'd love lights out by midnight. Could you use the lounge after that?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, no worries. I'll move to the lounge after twelve. And for chores, want to just alternate weeks?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll take this week's cleaning, you take next. Let's write it on the whiteboard so we don't forget.

questions.stem: What are the two roommates mainly discussing?

options.A: Whether to find a different apartment
options.B: How to set up rules for living together
options.C: Which classes they should take this term
options.D: A disagreement about a shared electricity bill

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕安排家务和安静时间的规则，B正确。A情境相反，他们是在协商同住而非搬走，属于reversed_situation。C从未提及，属于wrong_topic。D账单话题完全没出现，属于wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do after midnight on study nights?

options.A: Go to sleep at the same time as the woman
options.B: Ask the woman to study somewhere else
options.D: Study in the lounge instead of the room

questions.explanation: 男士同意午夜后去休息室学习，D正确。A与他夜猫子习惯不符且无依据，属于unrelated_next_step。B情境相反，是他自己让步去别处，属于reversed_situation。C过度推断，他只是换地点而非不再熬夜，属于over_inference。

items.situation: two students figuring out a time to meet for a group project

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We really need to meet about the history project. When are you free this week?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, Monday and Tuesday are packed with labs. How about Wednesday afternoon?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wednesday I have work until six. Could we do it then, like six-thirty?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, the library closes early on Wednesdays. Let's just meet Thursday at three instead.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Thursday at three works great. I'll book a study room and message the others.

questions.stem: When do the students finally agree to meet?
questions.explanation: 两人最终定在周四下午三点，C正确。A周一被排除因为有实验课，属于wrong_detail。B周三晚被否决因为图书馆早关门，属于wrong_detail。D周二也满是实验课，属于wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Reserve a study room and contact the group
options.C: Ask the professor for an extension
options.D: Finish the project entirely on her own

questions.explanation: 女士说她会订一个学习室并通知其他人，A正确。B无依据，她没说要请假，属于unrelated_next_step。C延期话题从未出现，属于unrelated_next_step。D过度推断，这是小组项目，她没打算独自完成，属于over_inference。

items.situation: a student returning a too-small sweater at a store counter

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I bought this wool sweater last weekend, but it's a medium and it's way too tight on me.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No problem at all. Do you have your receipt with you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I do, right here. I was hoping to exchange it for a large in the same color.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check the stock. Hmm, we're out of large in navy here, but our downtown branch has two in stock.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh. Could you have one shipped here instead? I'd rather not make the trip downtown.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Of course. I'll order it to this store, and we'll text you when it arrives, usually within three days.

questions.stem: Why does the woman come to the store?

options.A: To ask for a full cash refund
options.B: To buy a sweater as a gift
options.D: To exchange a sweater for a bigger size

questions.explanation: 女士因毛衣太小想换成大号，D正确。A情境相反，她要的是换货不是退款，属于reversed_situation。B从未提及买礼物，属于wrong_topic。C她没抱怨质量问题，只是尺码太小，属于wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do after this conversation?

options.A: Drive to the downtown branch herself
options.B: Wait for a text that the sweater has arrived
options.C: Choose a different color of sweater
options.D: Return the sweater for store credit

questions.explanation: 店员说会把货订到这家店并发短信通知，女士同意，所以她会等短信，B正确。A情境相反，她明确说不想去市区，属于rejected_option。C她坚持要同色，属于rejected_option。D她要换货而非退积分，属于over_inference。

items.situation: two students planning what cheese and food to buy for a dinner party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, for Saturday's dinner I want a really nice cheese board as the centerpiece. Any ideas?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, a sharp cheddar and a creamy brie usually please everyone. But remember Dana's lactose intolerant.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, right! I totally forgot. So we'd need something dairy-free for her too.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. There's a cashew-based cheese at the co-op that's surprisingly good. We could add some olives and crackers around it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. So cheddar, brie, the cashew one, plus olives and crackers. That covers everybody.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sounds great. The co-op closes at eight, so let's swing by right after our four o'clock class.

questions.stem: What is the conversation mainly about?

options.A: Deciding what foods to serve at a dinner party
options.B: Choosing a restaurant for Saturday night
options.C: Planning the guest list for a party
options.D: Learning how to make cheese at home

questions.explanation: 两人整段在商量晚宴要买的奶酪和配菜，A正确。B情境相反，他们是在家办晚宴而非去餐厅，属于reversed_situation。C只提到一位客人但重点是食物，属于too_narrow。D从未谈到自制奶酪，属于wrong_topic。

questions.stem: Why do they decide to include the cashew-based cheese?

options.A: Because it is the cheapest option at the co-op
options.B: Because the man prefers it to cheddar
options.C: So that a guest who can't eat dairy has a choice
options.D: Because the other cheeses were sold out

questions.explanation: 他们想到Dana乳糖不耐受，需要无乳制品的选择，所以加坚果奶酪，C正确。A价格从未提及，属于unrelated_next_step。B男士没说更喜欢它，属于over_inference。D其他奶酪并非售罄，属于wrong_detail。
